source_name: The Australia Institute - Taxing gas in Australia and Japan (Denniss, Campbell & Saunders, Apr 2026)
notes: The Australia Institute report "Taxing gas in Australia and Japan" by Richard Denniss, Rod Campbell and Matt Saunders, published 21 April 2026. Headline finding: Japan's gas tax raises more revenue from Australian gas exports than Australia's PRRT does in total. Japan collects A$710 million per year specifically taxing gas imports from Australia, and A$1.8 billion per year on gas imports overall, while Australia's PRRT raises A$1.4 billion per year (matches the canonical ATO 2023-24 PRRT details total of A$1,483.3m).
source_rights: CC BY 4.0 (The Australia Institute, where stated)
citation: The Australia Institute, Australian LNG export royalty analysis.

fields.report_title: Taxing gas in Australia and Japan
fields.authors: Richard Denniss; Rod Campbell; Matt Saunders
fields.comparison_finding: Japan raises more revenue taxing Australian gas imports than Australia raises from PRRT in total
